title: Identification of Root Nodule Isolates by 16S-rRNA PCR
author: Maluk, M.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The 16S rRNA gene is routinely used to identify bacteria to the generic level. This SOP describes how DNA is extracted from pure rhizobial cultures, and how the 16S rRNA gene is amplified from the DNA and then sequenced to allow for its identification by comparing it with sequences deposited in the Genbank database. The proper identification of rhizobia is essential if they are to be subject to further genetic and functional analyses, such as their performance at Biological Nitrogen Fixation (BNF) in symbiosis with compatible legumes. Identification at the generic level using 16S rRNA sequences is, however, only the first step in the full characterisation of rhizobial strains which will be necessary should they be selected as potential inoculants for commercial use. Therefore, this SOP also provides information about how other relevant genes ( recA ,  nodA ,  nodC ,  nifH ) can be amplified in order to provide more information about strains of interest.
